title: Brokers
description: Review individual applications for insurance to evaluate degree of risk involved and determine acceptance of applications. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of sales workers other than retail sales workers. May perform duties such as budgeting, accounting, and personnel work, in addition to supervisory duties. Sell life, property, casualty, health, automotive, or other types of insurance. May refer clients to independent brokers, work as an independent broker, or be employed by an insurance company. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Call on policyholders to deliver and explain policies, analyze insurance programs, suggest additions or changes, or change beneficiaries.
Attend meetings, seminars, and programs to learn about new products, services, and skills, and receive technical assistance in developing new accounts.
Calculate premiums, establish payment methods, and ensure policy requirements are fulfilled, including necessary examinations and appropriate forms.
Authorize reinsurance of policies when risks are high, and decline excessive risks.

Review company records to determine the amount of insurance in force on single risks or groups of closely related risks, and evaluate the possibility of losses due to catastrophe or excessive insurance.
Decrease the value of policies when risks are substandard, and apply necessary endorsements or ratings to ensure safe and profitable risk distribution, using reference materials.
Examine documents and interview applicants to determine the degree of risk from factors such as health, financial standing, and property condition.
Develop marketing strategies to compete in the insurance market and explain features, advantages, and disadvantages of various policies to promote sales.
Sell various types of insurance policies to businesses and individuals, including life, health, automotive, and specialized policies such as marine or medical malpractice.
Seek out new clients and develop clientele by networking and generating lists of prospective clients.
Customize insurance programs to suit individual customers, often covering a variety of risks, and discuss financial options with clients.
Contact underwriters and submit forms to obtain binder coverage, and confer with clients to provide information when claims are made.
Monitor insurance claims to ensure equitable settlements for both the client and the insurer, and perform administrative tasks such as maintaining records and handling policy renewals.
Select insurance companies that offer the requested coverage to underwrite policies, and report on positions or trading results when applicable.
Identify opportunities for the purchase or sale of securities or commodities and provide financial advice on such transactions.
Keep accurate records of transactions and prepare sales order tickets for processing of client-requested transactions.
Monitor or record locations of goods in transit, and track delivery progress of shipments.
Advise clients on transportation and payment methods, and negotiate shipping rates with freight carriers.
Prepare financial reports to monitor client or corporate finances and ensure compliance with regulations regarding transactions.
Listen to and resolve customer complaints regarding services, products, or personnel, and provide detailed information to clients regarding policy options and factors affecting their selections.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel, and direct and supervise employees engaged in insurance sales activities.
Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities with other departments, and plan and prepare work schedules for sales staff.
Monitor sales staff performance to ensure goals are met and provide assistance in performing complex tasks.
Follow up with clients and companies to ensure proper execution of contracts and resolution of issues related to policies.
